summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on three zoning cases. They recommended approval of rezoning the former Combs dump site from M2 Heavy Manufacturing to B2 General Business District, approved a text amendment removing the 2,000 square foot maximum for accessory structures in residential districts while retaining the 7% lot area cap, and approved a text amendment to PDM (Planned Development Mixed Use) standards requiring a minimum 20% non-residential floor area and phasing requirements to ensure commercial development accompanies residential construction.
decisions: Recommended approval of ZC 2026-002 to rezone Combs dump site parcels (112208F103, 112208F114, 112208F106/107) from M2 Heavy Manufacturing to B2 General Business District
Recommended approval of ZC 2026-003 text amendment to remove 2,000 sq ft maximum aggregate floor area for accessory structures in residential districts, retaining 7% lot area cap
Recommended approval of ZC 2026-004 text amendment to Article 6.02 regarding PDM development standards and minimum non-residential requirements
action_items: Land use plan rough draft to be presented at trustee meeting on April 14th
Commission resignation and replacement appointment to be addressed at next trustee session
M3 text amendment to be prepared for May 5, 2026 meeting agenda
Final cleanup of Table 6-1A and formal adoption tying it into Article 6 for PDM standards
key_amounts: $650,000 invested in Combs dump site brownfield cleanup (local money and Township ARPA funds)
7% of lot area cap for accessory structures in residential districts
40% minimum residential floor area required in PDM projects
20% minimum non-residential floor area required in PDM projects
1,400 sq ft assumed unit size for single family homes in PDM calculations
700 sq ft assumed unit size for townhome/multifamily/senior units in PDM calculations
60% residential completion threshold before non-residential construction must begin in PDM projects
notable_topics: Rezoning former Combs dump site from M2 to B2 for commercial redevelopment
Brownfield cleanup and redevelopment of former dump site owned by Clermont County Land Bank
Removal of 2,000 sq ft hard cap on accessory structures in residential zones
PDM mixed-use development standards to prevent projects from becoming mostly residential
Phasing requirements for non-residential construction in PDM developments
Land use plan update nearing completion
attendees: Mr. Frisbee (absent/no response at roll call)
notes: Mr. Frisbee was called during roll call but did not respond and appears absent. All three zoning cases passed unanimously. The transcript contains significant sections of garbled/corrupted text likely due to transcription errors. The Clermont County Land Bank owns the Combs dump site and is seeking the rezoning to attract commercial buyers. A commission member has resigned and the replacement process is pending. Next meeting scheduled for May 5, 2026 at 6:30 PM. Meeting adjourned at 7:02 PM.
title: ZC 4-7-26
